notes: Recording police in public is generally allowed in India as long as it does not interfere with their duties.
Home CCTV is generally legal in India when used for security, but privacy considerations apply if cameras capture others without consent.
Employee monitoring is generally allowed in India for business purposes, but should not be excessive or violate privacy expectations.
Audio recording laws in India are not always clearly defined, but recording conversations without consent can raise privacy and legal concerns.
Recording video in public is generally allowed in India, but misuse of footage or invasion of privacy can lead to legal issues.
Recording phone calls in India is not clearly defined in all cases, but recording without consent can raise privacy and legal concerns.
GPS tracking laws in India are context-based, but tracking someone without consent can lead to legal issues, especially if linked to harassment.
Drone use is regulated in India, and using drones for surveillance is restricted depending on location, permissions, and purpose.
Business CCTV is widely used in India for security and operational purposes, but must not violate privacy or labor rights.
Hidden cameras are not explicitly banned in all cases in India, but using them to secretly record individuals in private spaces can be illegal.
